label: Day 7 — Morning: Digital Escape Room Co-design
description: The most creative session of the exchange. Teams co-design the Digital Escape Room — the project's secondary output — built around the sustainability theme and structured as four 'rooms', one per country.
objectives: Understand the structure and logic of a digital escape room
Co-design a country-specific room with narrative, enigma, and solution
Connect local green practices to engaging interactive content
Produce room design documents ready for Genially implementation

steps.title: Escape Room anatomy
steps.body: Facilitator walks through the structure: setting/narrative → clue/challenge → solution → unlock. Shows a 2-minute demo of an existing Genially escape room.

steps.title: The NORTH Escape Room concept
steps.body: Group agrees on the overarching narrative: 'You are an environmental detective crossing Europe on a mission to stop a corporation from burying toxic waste in four countries. Each country holds a clue.'

steps.title: Room design sprint
steps.body: Each national team designs their room: setting (where in their country?), narrative hook, the enigma (puzzle based on a real green fact), the solution and unlock mechanism, and one visual element.

steps.title: Room previews
steps.body: Each team presents their room concept in 2 minutes. Group votes on one element they love and one suggestion for improvement.

facilitatorNote: The narrative frame ('environmental detective') makes the design process much easier because it gives teams a consistent logic to work within. Real stories — a polluted river, a disappearing forest, a community garden — always produce better enigmas than invented ones.
